mn9:1.2: at one time the *Bhagavā* was dwelling at Sāvatthī, in Jeta's Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika's park.
mn9:10.2: When, friends, a noble disciple understands nutriment, understands the arising of nutriment, understands the cessation of nutriment, and understands the path leading to the cessation of nutriment —
mn9:10.3: to that extent, friends, a noble disciple is one of right view, whose view is straight, who is endowed with verified confidence in the *Dhamma*, and who has arrived at this true *Dhamma*.
mn9:11.1: And what, friends, is nutriment, what is the arising of nutriment, what is the cessation of nutriment, what is the path leading to the cessation of nutriment?
mn9:11.2: There are, friends, four nutriments for the sustenance of beings that have come into existence, or for the support of those seeking birth.
mn9:11.4: Physical food, coarse or fine, is the first; contact is the second; mental volition is the third; consciousness [*viññāṇa*] is the fourth.
mn9:11.5: With the arising of *taṇhā* comes the arising of nutriment; with the cessation of *taṇhā* comes the cessation of nutriment; and this very noble eightfold path is the path leading to the cessation of nutriment, namely —
mn9:11.6: right view, right intention, right speech, right action, right livelihood, right effort, right *sati*, right *samādhi*.
mn9:12.1: When, friends, a noble disciple thus understands nutriment, thus understands the arising of nutriment, thus understands the cessation of nutriment, thus understands the path leading to the cessation of nutriment, then, having entirely abandoned the underlying tendency to lust, having dispelled the underlying tendency to aversion, having uprooted the underlying tendency to the conceit 'I am', having abandoned *avijjā* [*avijjā*] and aroused knowledge, they become one who makes an end of *dukkha* in this very life —
mn9:12.2: to that extent, friends, a noble disciple is one of right view, whose view is straight, who is endowed with verified confidence in the *Dhamma*, and who has arrived at this true *Dhamma*."
mn9:13.1: "Good, friend," those *bhikkhus*, having delighted and rejoiced in venerable Sāriputta's words, asked venerable Sāriputta a further question:
mn9:13.2: "But might there be, friend, another way in which a noble disciple is one of right view, whose view is straight, who is endowed with verified confidence in the *Dhamma*, and who has arrived at this true *Dhamma*?"
mn9:14.2: When, friends, a noble disciple understands *dukkha*, understands the arising of *dukkha*, understands the cessation of *dukkha*, and understands the path leading to the cessation of *dukkha* —
mn9:14.3: to that extent, friends, a noble disciple is one of right view, whose view is straight, who is endowed with verified confidence in the *Dhamma*, and who has arrived at this true *Dhamma*.
mn9:15.1: And what, friends, is *dukkha*, what is the arising of *dukkha*, what is the cessation of *dukkha*, what is the path leading to the cessation of *dukkha*?
mn9:15.2: Birth is *dukkha*, aging is *dukkha*, death is *dukkha*, sorrow, lamentation, pain, grief, and despair are *dukkha*, association with what is disliked is *dukkha*, separation from what is liked is *dukkha*, not getting what one wants is *dukkha*; in brief, the five aggregates of *upādāna* are *dukkha* —
mn9:16.1: And what, friends, is the arising of *dukkha*?
mn9:16.2: It is this *taṇhā* that leads to renewed *bhava*, accompanied by delight and lust, delighting here and there — namely,
mn9:16.3: *taṇhā* for sensual pleasures, *taṇhā* for *bhava*, *taṇhā* for non-existence —
mn9:16.4: this, friends, is called the arising of *dukkha*.
mn9:17.1: And what, friends, is the cessation of *dukkha*?
mn9:17.2: It is the remainderless fading away and cessation of that very *taṇhā*, the giving up, the relinquishing, the release, the non-attachment —
mn9:17.3: this, friends, is called the cessation of *dukkha*.
mn9:18.1: And what, friends, is the path leading to the cessation of *dukkha*?
mn9:18.2: It is this very noble eightfold path, namely —
mn9:18.4: this, friends, is called the path leading to the cessation of *dukkha*.
mn9:19.1: When, friends, a noble disciple thus understands *dukkha*, thus understands the arising of *dukkha*, thus understands the cessation of *dukkha*, thus understands the path leading to the cessation of *dukkha*, then, having entirely abandoned the underlying tendency to lust, having dispelled the underlying tendency to aversion, having uprooted the underlying tendency to the conceit 'I am', having abandoned *avijjā* [*avijjā*] and aroused knowledge, they become one who makes an end of *dukkha* in this very life —
mn9:19.2: to that extent, friends, a noble disciple is one of right view, whose view is straight, who is endowed with verified confidence in the *Dhamma*, and who has arrived at this true *Dhamma*."
mn9:2.1: "'Right view, right view,' friends, it is said.
mn9:2.2: In what way, friends, is a noble disciple one of right view, whose view is straight, who is endowed with verified confidence in the *Dhamma*, and who has arrived at this true *Dhamma*?"
mn9:2.3: "We would come from afar, friend, to learn the meaning of this statement in the presence of venerable Sāriputta.
mn9:2.4: It would be good if venerable Sāriputta himself would explain the meaning of this statement.
mn9:2.5: Having heard it from venerable Sāriputta, the *bhikkhus* will remember it."
mn9:2.6: "Then, friends, listen and pay close attention; I shall speak."
mn9:2.7: "Yes, friend," those *bhikkhus* replied to venerable Sāriputta.
mn9:20.1: "Good, friend," those *bhikkhus*, having delighted and rejoiced in venerable Sāriputta's words, asked venerable Sāriputta a further question:
mn9:20.2: "But might there be, friend, another way in which a noble disciple is one of right view, whose view is straight, who is endowed with verified confidence in the *Dhamma*, and who has arrived at this true *Dhamma*?"
mn9:21.2: When, friends, a noble disciple understands aging-and-death, understands the arising of aging-and-death, understands the cessation of aging-and-death, and understands the path leading to the cessation of aging-and-death —
mn9:21.3: to that extent, friends, a noble disciple is one of right view, whose view is straight, who is endowed with verified confidence in the *Dhamma*, and who has arrived at this true *Dhamma*.
mn9:22.1: And what, friends, is aging-and-death, what is the arising of aging-and-death, what is the cessation of aging-and-death, what is the path leading to the cessation of aging-and-death?
mn9:22.2: Whatever aging, decrepitude, broken teeth, grey hair, wrinkled skin, diminishing of lifespan, ripening of the faculties there is among the various beings of the various orders of beings —
mn9:22.5: Whatever passing away, perishing, breaking up, disappearance, dying, death, completion of time, breaking up of the aggregates, laying down of the body, cutting off of the life faculty there is among the various beings of the various orders of beings —
mn9:22.7: Thus this aging and this death —
mn9:22.9: With the arising of birth comes the arising of aging-and-death; with the cessation of birth comes the cessation of aging-and-death; and this very noble eightfold path is the path leading to the cessation of aging-and-death, namely —
mn9:23.1: When, friends, a noble disciple thus understands aging-and-death, thus understands the arising of aging-and-death, thus understands the cessation of aging-and-death, thus understands the path leading to the cessation of aging-and-death, then, having entirely abandoned the underlying tendency to lust … they become one who makes an end of *dukkha* in this very life —
mn9:23.2: to that extent, friends, a noble disciple is one of right view, whose view is straight, who is endowed with verified confidence in the *Dhamma*, and who has arrived at this true *Dhamma*."
mn9:25.2: When, friends, a noble disciple understands birth, understands the arising of birth, understands the cessation of birth, and understands the path leading to the cessation of birth —
mn9:25.3: to that extent, friends, a noble disciple is one of right view, whose view is straight, who is endowed with verified confidence in the *Dhamma*, and who has arrived at this true *Dhamma*.
mn9:26.1: And what, friends, is birth, what is the arising of birth, what is the cessation of birth, what is the path leading to the cessation of birth?
mn9:26.2: Whatever birth, being born, descent, coming into existence, appearance of the aggregates, acquisition of the dimensions [*āyatana*] there is among the various beings of the various orders of beings —
mn9:26.4: With the arising of *bhava* comes the arising of birth; with the cessation of *bhava* comes the cessation of birth; and this very noble eightfold path is the path leading to the cessation of birth, namely —
mn9:27.1: When, friends, a noble disciple thus understands birth, thus understands the arising of birth, thus understands the cessation of birth, thus understands the path leading to the cessation of birth, then, having entirely abandoned the underlying tendency to lust … they become one who makes an end of *dukkha* in this very life —
mn9:27.2: to that extent, friends, a noble disciple is one of right view, whose view is straight, who is endowed with verified confidence in the *Dhamma*, and who has arrived at this true *Dhamma*."
mn9:29.2: When, friends, a noble disciple understands *bhava*, understands the arising of *bhava*, understands the cessation of *bhava*, and understands the path leading to the cessation of *bhava* —
mn9:29.3: to that extent, friends, a noble disciple is one of right view, whose view is straight, who is endowed with verified confidence in the *Dhamma*, and who has arrived at this true *Dhamma*.
mn9:3.1: "When, friends, a noble disciple understands the unwholesome and understands the root of the unwholesome, understands the wholesome and understands the root of the wholesome —
mn9:3.2: to that extent, friends, a noble disciple is one of right view, whose view is straight, who is endowed with verified confidence in the *Dhamma*, and who has arrived at this true *Dhamma*.
mn9:30.1: And what, friends, is *bhava*, what is the arising of *bhava*, what is the cessation of *bhava*, what is the path leading to the cessation of *bhava*?
mn9:30.2: There are, friends, three kinds of *bhava* —
mn9:30.4: With the arising of *upādāna* comes the arising of *bhava*; with the cessation of *upādāna* comes the cessation of *bhava*; and this very noble eightfold path is the path leading to the cessation of *bhava*, namely —
mn9:31.1: When, friends, a noble disciple thus understands *bhava*, thus understands the arising of *bhava*, thus understands the cessation of *bhava*, thus understands the path leading to the cessation of *bhava*, then, having entirely abandoned the underlying tendency to lust … they become one who makes an end of *dukkha* in this very life.
mn9:31.2: To that extent, friends, a noble disciple is one of right view, whose view is straight, who is endowed with verified confidence in the *Dhamma*, and who has arrived at this true *Dhamma*."
mn9:33.2: When, friends, a noble disciple understands *upādāna*, understands the arising of *upādāna*, understands the cessation of *upādāna*, and understands the path leading to the cessation of *upādāna* —
mn9:33.3: to that extent, friends, a noble disciple is one of right view, whose view is straight, who is endowed with verified confidence in the *Dhamma*, and who has arrived at this true *Dhamma*.
mn9:34.1: And what, friends, is *upādāna*, what is the arising of *upādāna*, what is the cessation of *upādāna*, what is the path leading to the cessation of *upādāna*?
mn9:34.2: There are, friends, four kinds of *upādāna* —
mn9:34.4: With the arising of *taṇhā* comes the arising of *upādāna*; with the cessation of *taṇhā* comes the cessation of *upādāna*; and this very noble eightfold path is the path leading to the cessation of *upādāna*, namely —
mn9:35.1: When, friends, a noble disciple thus understands *upādāna*, thus understands the arising of *upādāna*, thus understands the cessation of *upādāna*, thus understands the path leading to the cessation of *upādāna*, then, having entirely abandoned the underlying tendency to lust … they become one who makes an end of *dukkha* in this very life —
mn9:35.2: to that extent, friends, a noble disciple is one of right view, whose view is straight, who is endowed with verified confidence in the *Dhamma*, and who has arrived at this true *Dhamma*."
mn9:37.2: When, friends, a noble disciple understands *taṇhā*, understands the arising of *taṇhā*, understands the cessation of *taṇhā*, and understands the path leading to the cessation of *taṇhā* —
mn9:37.3: to that extent, friends, a noble disciple is one of right view, whose view is straight, who is endowed with verified confidence in the *Dhamma*, and who has arrived at this true *Dhamma*.
mn9:38.1: And what, friends, is *taṇhā*, what is the arising of *taṇhā*, what is the cessation of *taṇhā*, what is the path leading to the cessation of *taṇhā*?
mn9:38.2: There are, friends, these six bodies of *taṇhā* —
mn9:38.3: *taṇhā* for forms [*rūpa*], *taṇhā* for sounds, *taṇhā* for smells, *taṇhā* for tastes, *taṇhā* for touches, *taṇhā* for *dhammas*.
mn9:38.4: With the arising of *vedanā* comes the arising of *taṇhā*; with the cessation of *vedanā* comes the cessation of *taṇhā*; and this very noble eightfold path is the path leading to the cessation of *taṇhā*, namely —
mn9:39.1: When, friends, a noble disciple thus understands *taṇhā*, thus understands the arising of *taṇhā*, thus understands the cessation of *taṇhā*, thus understands the path leading to the cessation of *taṇhā*, then, having entirely abandoned the underlying tendency to lust … they become one who makes an end of *dukkha* in this very life —
mn9:39.2: to that extent, friends, a noble disciple is one of right view, whose view is straight, who is endowed with verified confidence in the *Dhamma*, and who has arrived at this true *Dhamma*."
mn9:4.1: And what, friends, is the unwholesome, what is the root of the unwholesome, what is the wholesome, what is the root of the wholesome?
mn9:4.2: Killing living beings, friends, is unwholesome; taking what is not given is unwholesome; misconduct in sensual pleasures is unwholesome; false speech is unwholesome; divisive speech is unwholesome; harsh speech is unwholesome; idle chatter is unwholesome; covetousness is unwholesome; ill will is unwholesome; wrong view is unwholesome —
mn9:4.3: this, friends, is called the unwholesome.
mn9:41.2: When, friends, a noble disciple understands *vedanā*, understands the arising of *vedanā*, understands the cessation of *vedanā*, and understands the path leading to the cessation of *vedanā* —
mn9:41.3: to that extent, friends, a noble disciple is one of right view, whose view is straight, who is endowed with verified confidence in the *Dhamma*, and who has arrived at this true *Dhamma*.
mn9:42.1: And what, friends, is *vedanā*, what is the arising of *vedanā*, what is the cessation of *vedanā*, what is the path leading to the cessation of *vedanā*?
mn9:42.2: There are, friends, these six bodies of *vedanā* —
mn9:42.3: *vedanā* born of eye-contact, *vedanā* born of ear-contact, *vedanā* born of nose-contact, *vedanā* born of tongue-contact, *vedanā* born of body-contact, *vedanā* born of mind-contact.
mn9:42.4: With the arising of contact comes the arising of *vedanā*; with the cessation of contact comes the cessation of *vedanā*; and this very noble eightfold path is the path leading to the cessation of *vedanā*, namely —
mn9:43.1: When, friends, a noble disciple thus understands *vedanā*, thus understands the arising of *vedanā*, thus understands the cessation of *vedanā*, thus understands the path leading to the cessation of *vedanā*, then, having entirely abandoned the underlying tendency to lust … they become one who makes an end of *dukkha* in this very life —
mn9:43.2: to that extent, friends, a noble disciple is one of right view, whose view is straight, who is endowed with verified confidence in the *Dhamma*, and who has arrived at this true *Dhamma*."
mn9:45.2: When, friends, a noble disciple understands contact, understands the arising of contact, understands the cessation of contact, and understands the path leading to the cessation of contact —
mn9:45.3: to that extent, friends, a noble disciple is one of right view, whose view is straight, who is endowed with verified confidence in the *Dhamma*, and who has arrived at this true *Dhamma*.
mn9:46.1: And what, friends, is contact, what is the arising of contact, what is the cessation of contact, what is the path leading to the cessation of contact?
mn9:46.2: There are, friends, these six bodies of contact —
mn9:46.3: eye-contact, ear-contact, nose-contact, tongue-contact, body-contact, mind-contact.
mn9:46.4: With the arising of the *saḷāyatana* comes the arising of contact; with the cessation of the *saḷāyatana* comes the cessation of contact; and this very noble eightfold path is the path leading to the cessation of contact, namely —
mn9:47.1: When, friends, a noble disciple thus understands contact, thus understands the arising of contact, thus understands the cessation of contact, thus understands the path leading to the cessation of contact, then, having entirely abandoned the underlying tendency to lust … they become one who makes an end of *dukkha* in this very life —
mn9:47.2: to that extent, friends, a noble disciple is one of right view, whose view is straight, who is endowed with verified confidence in the *Dhamma*, and who has arrived at this true *Dhamma*."
mn9:49.2: When, friends, a noble disciple understands the *saḷāyatana*, understands the arising of the *saḷāyatana*, understands the cessation of the *saḷāyatana*, and understands the path leading to the cessation of the *saḷāyatana* —
mn9:49.3: to that extent, friends, a noble disciple is one of right view, whose view is straight, who is endowed with verified confidence in the *Dhamma*, and who has arrived at this true *Dhamma*.
mn9:5.1: And what, friends, is the root of the unwholesome?
mn9:5.2: Greed is a root of the unwholesome, hatred is a root of the unwholesome, delusion is a root of the unwholesome —
mn9:5.3: this, friends, is called the root of the unwholesome.
mn9:50.1: And what, friends, is the *saḷāyatana*, what is the arising of the *saḷāyatana*, what is the cessation of the *saḷāyatana*, what is the path leading to the cessation of the *saḷāyatana*?
mn9:50.2: There are, friends, these six dimensions [*āyatana*] —
mn9:50.3: the eye dimension [*āyatana*], the ear dimension [*āyatana*], the nose dimension [*āyatana*], the tongue dimension [*āyatana*], the body dimension [*āyatana*], the mind dimension [*āyatana*].
mn9:50.4: With the arising of *nāmarūpa* comes the arising of the *saḷāyatana*; with the cessation of *nāmarūpa* comes the cessation of the *saḷāyatana*; and this very noble eightfold path is the path leading to the cessation of the *saḷāyatana*, namely —
mn9:51.1: When, friends, a noble disciple thus understands the *saḷāyatana*, thus understands the arising of the *saḷāyatana*, thus understands the cessation of the *saḷāyatana*, thus understands the path leading to the cessation of the *saḷāyatana*, then, having entirely abandoned the underlying tendency to lust … they become one who makes an end of *dukkha* in this very life —
mn9:51.2: to that extent, friends, a noble disciple is one of right view, whose view is straight, who is endowed with verified confidence in the *Dhamma*, and who has arrived at this true *Dhamma*."
mn9:53.2: When, friends, a noble disciple understands *nāmarūpa*, understands the arising of *nāmarūpa*, understands the cessation of *nāmarūpa*, and understands the path leading to the cessation of *nāmarūpa* —
mn9:53.3: to that extent, friends, a noble disciple is one of right view, whose view is straight, who is endowed with verified confidence in the *Dhamma*, and who has arrived at this true *Dhamma*.
mn9:54.1: And what, friends, is *nāmarūpa*, what is the arising of *nāmarūpa*, what is the cessation of *nāmarūpa*, what is the path leading to the cessation of *nāmarūpa*?
mn9:54.4: the four great elements, and the form [*rūpa*] derived from the four great elements —
mn9:54.6: Thus this name and this form [*rūpa*] —
mn9:54.8: With the arising of *viññāṇa* comes the arising of *nāmarūpa*; with the cessation of *viññāṇa* comes the cessation of *nāmarūpa*; and this very noble eightfold path is the path leading to the cessation of *nāmarūpa*, namely —
mn9:55.1: When, friends, a noble disciple thus understands *nāmarūpa*, thus understands the arising of *nāmarūpa*, thus understands the cessation of *nāmarūpa*, thus understands the path leading to the cessation of *nāmarūpa*, then, having entirely abandoned the underlying tendency to lust … they become one who makes an end of *dukkha* in this very life —
mn9:55.2: to that extent, friends, a noble disciple is one of right view, whose view is straight, who is endowed with verified confidence in the *Dhamma*, and who has arrived at this true *Dhamma*."
mn9:57.2: When, friends, a noble disciple understands *viññāṇa*, understands the arising of *viññāṇa*, understands the cessation of *viññāṇa*, and understands the path leading to the cessation of *viññāṇa* —
mn9:57.3: to that extent, friends, a noble disciple is one of right view, whose view is straight, who is endowed with verified confidence in the *Dhamma*, and who has arrived at this true *Dhamma*.
mn9:58.1: And what, friends, is *viññāṇa*, what is the arising of *viññāṇa*, what is the cessation of *viññāṇa*, what is the path leading to the cessation of *viññāṇa*?
mn9:58.2: There are, friends, these six bodies of *viññāṇa* —
mn9:58.3: eye-*viññāṇa*, ear-*viññāṇa*, nose-*viññāṇa*, tongue-*viññāṇa*, body-*viññāṇa*, mind-*viññāṇa*.
mn9:58.4: With the arising of *saṅkhāras* comes the arising of *viññāṇa*; with the cessation of *saṅkhāras* comes the cessation of *viññāṇa*; and this very noble eightfold path is the path leading to the cessation of *viññāṇa*, namely —
mn9:59.1: When, friends, a noble disciple thus understands *viññāṇa*, thus understands the arising of *viññāṇa*, thus understands the cessation of *viññāṇa*, thus understands the path leading to the cessation of *viññāṇa*, then, having entirely abandoned the underlying tendency to lust … they become one who makes an end of *dukkha* in this very life —
mn9:59.2: to that extent, friends, a noble disciple is one of right view, whose view is straight, who is endowed with verified confidence in the *Dhamma*, and who has arrived at this true *Dhamma*."
mn9:6.1: And what, friends, is the wholesome?
mn9:6.2: Refraining from killing living beings is wholesome; refraining from taking what is not given is wholesome; refraining from misconduct in sensual pleasures is wholesome; refraining from false speech is wholesome; refraining from divisive speech is wholesome; refraining from harsh speech is wholesome; refraining from idle chatter is wholesome; non-covetousness is wholesome; non-ill will is wholesome; right view is wholesome —
mn9:6.3: this, friends, is called the wholesome.
mn9:61.2: When, friends, a noble disciple understands *saṅkhāras*, understands the arising of *saṅkhāras*, understands the cessation of *saṅkhāras*, and understands the path leading to the cessation of *saṅkhāras* —
mn9:61.3: to that extent, friends, a noble disciple is one of right view, whose view is straight, who is endowed with verified confidence in the *Dhamma*, and who has arrived at this true *Dhamma*.
mn9:62.1: And what, friends, are *saṅkhāras*, what is the arising of *saṅkhāras*, what is the cessation of *saṅkhāras*, what is the path leading to the cessation of *saṅkhāras*?
mn9:62.3: the bodily *saṅkhāra*, the verbal *saṅkhāra*, the mental *saṅkhāra*.
mn9:62.4: With the arising of *avijjā* [*avijjā*] comes the arising of *saṅkhāras*; with the cessation of *avijjā* [*avijjā*] comes the cessation of *saṅkhāras*; and this very noble eightfold path is the path leading to the cessation of *saṅkhāras*, namely —
mn9:63.1: When, friends, a noble disciple thus understands *saṅkhāras*, thus understands the arising of *saṅkhāras*, thus understands the cessation of *saṅkhāras*, thus understands the path leading to the cessation of *saṅkhāras*, then, having entirely abandoned the underlying tendency to lust, having dispelled the underlying tendency to aversion, having uprooted the underlying tendency to the conceit 'I am', having abandoned *avijjā* [*avijjā*] and aroused knowledge, they become one who makes an end of *dukkha* in this very life —
mn9:63.2: to that extent, friends, a noble disciple is one of right view, whose view is straight, who is endowed with verified confidence in the *Dhamma*, and who has arrived at this true *Dhamma*."
mn9:65.1: When, friends, a noble disciple understands *avijjā* [*avijjā*], understands the arising of *avijjā* [*avijjā*], understands the cessation of *avijjā* [*avijjā*], and understands the path leading to the cessation of *avijjā* [*avijjā*] —
mn9:65.2: to that extent, friends, a noble disciple is one of right view, whose view is straight, who is endowed with verified confidence in the *Dhamma*, and who has arrived at this true *Dhamma*.
mn9:65.3: And what, friends, is *avijjā* [*avijjā*], what is the arising of *avijjā* [*avijjā*], what is the cessation of *avijjā* [*avijjā*], what is the path leading to the cessation of *avijjā* [*avijjā*]?
mn9:65.4: Whatever non-knowledge regarding *dukkha*, non-knowledge regarding the arising of *dukkha*, non-knowledge regarding the cessation of *dukkha*, non-knowledge regarding the path leading to the cessation of *dukkha* —
mn9:66.1: With the arising of the *āsavas* comes the arising of *avijjā* [*avijjā*]; with the cessation of the *āsavas* comes the cessation of *avijjā* [*avijjā*]; and this very noble eightfold path is the path leading to the cessation of *avijjā* [*avijjā*], namely —
mn9:67.1: When, friends, a noble disciple thus understands *avijjā* [*avijjā*], thus understands the arising of *avijjā* [*avijjā*], thus understands the cessation of *avijjā* [*avijjā*], thus understands the path leading to the cessation of *avijjā* [*avijjā*], then, having entirely abandoned the underlying tendency to lust, having dispelled the underlying tendency to aversion, having uprooted the underlying tendency to the conceit 'I am', having abandoned *avijjā* [*avijjā*] and aroused knowledge, they become one who makes an end of *dukkha* in this very life —
mn9:67.2: to that extent, friends, a noble disciple is one of right view, whose view is straight, who is endowed with verified confidence in the *Dhamma*, and who has arrived at this true *Dhamma*."
mn9:68.1: "Good, friend," those *bhikkhus*, having delighted and rejoiced in venerable Sāriputta's words, asked venerable Sāriputta a further question:
mn9:68.2: "But might there be, friend, another way in which a noble disciple is one of right view, whose view is straight, who is endowed with verified confidence in the *Dhamma*, and who has arrived at this true *Dhamma*?"
mn9:69.2: When, friends, a noble disciple understands the *āsavas*, understands the arising of the *āsavas*, understands the cessation of the *āsavas*, and understands the path leading to the cessation of the *āsavas* —
mn9:69.3: to that extent, friends, a noble disciple is one of right view, whose view is straight, who is endowed with verified confidence in the *Dhamma*, and who has arrived at this true *Dhamma*.
mn9:7.1: And what, friends, is the root of the wholesome?
mn9:7.2: Non-greed is a root of the wholesome, non-hatred is a root of the wholesome, non-delusion is a root of the wholesome —
mn9:7.3: this, friends, is called the root of the wholesome.
mn9:70.1: And what, friends, are the *āsavas*, what is the arising of the *āsavas*, what is the cessation of the *āsavas*, what is the path leading to the cessation of the *āsavas*?
mn9:70.3: the *āsava* of sensual pleasure, the *āsava* of *bhava*, the *āsava* of *avijjā* [*avijjā*].
mn9:70.4: With the arising of *avijjā* [*avijjā*] comes the arising of the *āsavas*; with the cessation of *avijjā* [*avijjā*] comes the cessation of the *āsavas*; and this very noble eightfold path is the path leading to the cessation of the *āsavas*, namely —
mn9:71.1: When, friends, a noble disciple thus understands the *āsavas*, thus understands the arising of the *āsavas*, thus understands the cessation of the *āsavas*, thus understands the path leading to the cessation of the *āsavas*, then, having entirely abandoned the underlying tendency to lust, having dispelled the underlying tendency to aversion, having uprooted the underlying tendency to the conceit 'I am', having abandoned *avijjā* [*avijjā*] and aroused knowledge, they become one who makes an end of *dukkha* in this very life —
mn9:71.2: to that extent, friends, a noble disciple is one of right view, whose view is straight, who is endowed with verified confidence in the *Dhamma*, and who has arrived at this true *Dhamma*."
mn9:71.3: This is what venerable Sāriputta said.
mn9:71.4: Satisfied, those *bhikkhus* delighted in venerable Sāriputta's words.
mn9:71.5: Right View, the ninth, is finished.
mn9:8.1: When, friends, a noble disciple thus understands the unwholesome, thus understands the root of the unwholesome, thus understands the wholesome, thus understands the root of the wholesome, then, having entirely abandoned the underlying tendency to lust, having dispelled the underlying tendency to aversion, having uprooted the underlying tendency to the conceit 'I am', having abandoned *avijjā* [*avijjā*] and aroused knowledge, they become one who makes an end of *dukkha* in this very life —
mn9:8.2: to that extent, friends, a noble disciple is one of right view, whose view is straight, who is endowed with verified confidence in the *Dhamma*, and who has arrived at this true *Dhamma*."
mn9:9.1: "Good, friend," those *bhikkhus*, having delighted and rejoiced in venerable Sāriputta's words, asked venerable Sāriputta a further question:
mn9:9.2: "But might there be, friend, another way in which a noble disciple is one of right view, whose view is straight, who is endowed with verified confidence in the *Dhamma*, and who has arrived at this true *Dhamma*?"
